stops.name: Marienplatz
stops.shortDescription: The heart of Munich since 1158, home to the famous Glockenspiel.

stops.name: Frauenkirche
stops.shortDescription: Munich's iconic twin-domed cathedral with the Devil's Footprint legend.

stops.name: Odeonsplatz
stops.shortDescription: A large square at the northern edge of the old town, flanked by the impressive Feldherrnhalle and the bright yellow Theatine Church.

stops.name: Hofgarten
stops.shortDescription: A beautiful Italian-style Renaissance garden located between the Residenz and the English Garden, featuring a central pavilion dedicated to the goddess Diana.

stops.name: Munich Residenz
stops.shortDescription: The former royal palace of the Wittelsbach monarchs of Bavaria. It is the largest city palace in Germany, featuring spectacularly opulent rooms and courtyards.

stops.name: Nationaltheater
stops.shortDescription: An exquisite neo-classical opera house on Max-Joseph-Platz. It is the home of the world-renowned Bavarian State Opera and Ballet.

stops.name: Alter Hof
stops.shortDescription: The Old Court was the former medieval residence of Louis IV, Holy Roman Emperor. It provides a quiet, historic courtyard tucked right in the center of the city.

stops.name: Hofbräuhaus
stops.shortDescription: The most famous beer hall in the world, dating back to 1589. It offers an authentic Bavarian experience with massive steins of beer, pretzels, and live Oompah bands.

stops.name: Viktualienmarkt
stops.shortDescription: Munich's daily outdoor food market offering gourmet produce, local cheeses, meats, and flowers, along with one of the city's most central and traditional beer gardens.

stops.name: St. Peter's Church (Alter Peter)
stops.shortDescription: Known affectionately as 'Alter Peter', this is the oldest parish church in Munich. Climbing its tower offers arguably the best panoramic view of the old town.

stops.name: Asamkirche
stops.shortDescription: A stunningly ornate Baroque and Rococo church built by the Asam brothers in the 18th century as their private chapel, seamlessly integrated into a row of houses on Sendlinger Straße.
